title_en: The Final Days of the Prophet ﷺ
content_en:
The final chapter of al-Rahiq al-Makhtum is among the most emotionally powerful sections of any biography ever written. Al-Mubarakpuri draws on the most authentic accounts to reconstruct the Prophet's ﷺ last days with both scholarly precision and the depth of feeling appropriate to the loss that the death of the Prophet ﷺ represented — a loss the Quran itself had prepared the community to face: 'Muhammad is not but a messenger; other messengers have passed on before him. So if he was to die or be killed, would you turn back on your heels?' (Quran 3:144).

In the month of Safar of the eleventh year of Hijra (632 CE), the Prophet ﷺ went to the cemetery of al-Baqi' in the night and prayed for the deceased buried there — an event that his wife Aishah later recognized as the beginning of his farewell to the world. He returned home with a headache that would mark the beginning of his final illness. As the illness intensified, he obtained permission from his wives to be nursed in Aishah's apartment — his own small chamber adjoining the mosque.

Despite his increasing weakness, the Prophet ﷺ continued to lead the prayers for as long as he was able. When the illness became too severe, he instructed Abu Bakr to lead the prayers in his place — a designation of extraordinary significance, not lost on the Companions who observed it. On one occasion, finding himself slightly better, he came out and sat beside Abu Bakr in the prayer — Abu Bakr stepped back to yield, but the Prophet ﷺ gestured for him to remain leading while he prayed seated beside him.

In his final days, the Prophet ﷺ continued to offer guidance and counsel to his community. He repeatedly warned against the elevation of his own grave or anyone's grave to a place of worship: 'Allah cursed the Jews and the Christians for taking the graves of their prophets as places of worship. Do not take graves as places of worship — I forbid you from that.' He freed every slave he had and gave away every possession, leaving nothing of worldly goods.

On Monday the 12th of Rabi' al-Awwal (June 8, 632 CE), the Prophet ﷺ lifted the curtain of Aishah's room during the Fajr prayer and looked out at his Companions praying. He smiled — a smile so radiant that the Companions, seeing it, began to move toward him, thinking he was coming out. Abu Bakr, understanding it as a farewell smile, stepped forward to yield his place, but the Prophet ﷺ gestured for the prayer to continue and let the curtain fall. That would be his last view of his community in prayer.

Later that morning, in the arms of Aishah, with his head resting against her chest and her hand moistening his lips with water, the Prophet ﷺ murmured his final words: 'With the highest companion... with those upon whom Allah has bestowed His grace — of the prophets, the truthful, the martyrs, and the righteous... O Allah, the highest companion...' His head grew heavy. The Seal of the Prophets, the Mercy to all the worlds, had returned to his Lord. Aishah later said: 'If I were to choose the best of all deaths, I would choose the death he had — for he died between my chest and my neck, on the day allotted to me, in my apartment.' The community was shattered — Umar refused to believe it, standing outside and threatening anyone who said the Prophet ﷺ was dead. It was Abu Bakr who steadied them: 'O people, whoever worshipped Muhammad — Muhammad has died. But whoever worships Allah — Allah is Ever-Living and never dies.' Then he recited the verse from Quran 3:144. Umar collapsed when he heard it, as though the verse was being revealed for the first time.
content_id:
Bab terakhir dari Al-Rahiq al-Makhtum adalah salah satu bagian yang paling menggetarkan emosi dari biografi mana pun yang pernah ditulis. Al-Mubarakpuri menggambarkan hari-hari terakhir Nabi ﷺ dengan presisi ilmiah sekaligus kedalaman perasaan yang layak bagi kehilangan yang ditimbulkan oleh kematian Nabi ﷺ — kehilangan yang tak tertandingi dalam sejarah Islam.

Dimulai sekitar 2-3 bulan setelah Haji Wada', Nabi ﷺ mulai mengalami sakit kepala yang parah dan demam tinggi. Meskipun sakit, beliau tetap mengimami shalat para sahabat selama beberapa hari. Ketika kondisi beliau memburuk, beliau meminta Abu Bakr (ra) untuk mengimami shalat — sebuah indikasi jelas tentang siapa yang beliau kehendaki sebagai pemimpin umat setelah wafatnya.

Dalam hari-hari terakhirnya, Nabi ﷺ dirawat secara bergantian di rumah-rumah istri-istrinya. Atas izin mereka semua, beliau dipindahkan ke kamar Aisyah (ra) — tempat beliau menghembuskan napas terakhir. Beliau wafat pada hari Senin, 12 Rabi' Al-Awwal tahun 11 H (Juni 632 M), dengan kepala bersandar di pangkuan Aisyah (ra).

Kalimat-kalimat terakhir yang beliau ucapkan adalah: "Allahumma ar-Rafiq al-A'la" (Ya Allah, menuju teman sejati yang tertinggi). Ini merujuk pada pilihannya untuk menemani "kelompok yang Allah beri nikmat atas mereka dari para nabi, shiddiqin, syuhada, dan orang-orang saleh." (QS. An-Nisa': 69)

Umar bin Al-Khattab (ra) tidak dapat menerima kenyataan ini dan mengancam akan membunuh siapa pun yang mengatakan Nabi ﷺ telah wafat. Abu Bakr (ra) kemudian berdiri dan menyampaikan khutbah bersejarahnya: "Barangsiapa menyembah Muhammad, maka sesungguhnya Muhammad telah wafat. Dan barangsiapa menyembah Allah, maka sesungguhnya Allah Maha Hidup dan tidak akan pernah mati." Dan beliau membacakan: "Muhammad hanyalah seorang rasul..." (QS. Ali Imran: 144). Dengan itu, dunia Islam menatap ke depan, dan warisan Nabi ﷺ terus hidup.
content_ar:
الفصل الأخير من الرحيق المختوم من أشد فصول أي سيرة تأثيراً في الوجدان. ويستقي المباركفوري من أصح الروايات ليُعيد بناء أيام النبي ﷺ الأخيرة بدقة علمية ووجدان حيّ يُلائم فداحة الخسارة التي جاءت بعد ذلك.

بدأ المرض في صفر من السنة الحادية عشرة، وأخذ يشتد شيئاً فشيئاً. وكان النبي ﷺ يُصلي بالناس ما استطاع، حتى إذا اشتد به الوجع أمر أبا بكر أن يؤم الناس. وأسمه الناس: صداع الرأس مع الحمى. وبقي يُشرف على الأمر ما أمكنه، وفي أحد أيامه الأخيرة خرج وعلى رأسه عصابة فصعد المنبر وخطب الناس في آخر خطبة له.

وفي صبيحة الاثنين الثاني عشر من ربيع الأول سنة 11هـ، كشف النبي ﷺ ستر حجرة عائشة رضي الله عنها وهو ينظر إلى الصحابة وهم يصلون خلف أبي بكر، فأشرق وجهه ابتساماً. ثم أُرخي الستار وانتقل ﷺ إلى الرفيق الأعلى وقد اختار الله له ما عنده. وكانت المدينة تغصّ بالصحابة الذين لم يُصدّقوا النبأ وقد أفلت منهم شمسهم.
